title: Pyrus eminentoides

Pyrus communis is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a deciduous tree or shrub that is native to Europe and Asia. Pyrus communis has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis are tinctures, infusions, and teas, often used to treat digestive issues, insomnia, and anxiety.
